content:
# Luke Chapter 12 — Translation Disagreements

## Verse-by-Verse Disagreements

### 12:1 — Temporal connector
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "In which [time]" |
| LOGOS | "In the meantime" |
| PNEUMA | "Meanwhile" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"In the meantime"** (follows LOGOS — formal yet natural) |

### 12:1 — "Beware" / "Guard yourselves"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "Guard yourselves from" |
| LOGOS | "Beware of" |
| PNEUMA | "Be on your guard against" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Beware of"** (follows LOGOS — concise formal equivalent) |

### 12:4 — Clause after "kill the body"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "after these things not having anything more to do" |
| LOGOS | "after this have nothing more that they can do" |
| PNEUMA | "after that can do nothing more" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"after that have nothing more that they can do"** (follows LOGOS) |

### 12:8 — "confesses me" / "confess in me" / "acknowledges me"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "confess in me" (Semitic *en emoi*) |
| LOGOS | "confesses me" |
| PNEUMA | "acknowledges me" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"confesses me"** (follows LOGOS — natural English with confessional weight) |

### 12:12 — "needful" / "necessary" / "need"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "it is necessary" |
| LOGOS | "it is needful" |
| PNEUMA | "you need" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"it is needful"** (follows LOGOS — consistent with prior SOPHIA decisions) |

### 12:14 — Vocative address
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "Man" |
| LOGOS | "Man" |
| PNEUMA | "Friend" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Man"** (follows EMET/LOGOS 2-to-1 — direct vocative *anthrope*) |

### 12:15 — "covetousness" / "greed"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "covetousness" |
| LOGOS | "covetousness" |
| PNEUMA | "greed" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"covetousness"** (follows EMET/LOGOS 2-to-1 — theological term) |

### 12:15 — Life and possessions clause
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "not in the abounding of things to someone is his life out of his possessions" |
| LOGOS | "not in the abundance of one's possessions does his life consist" |
| PNEUMA | "a person's life does not consist in the abundance of their possessions" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"not in the abundance of one's possessions does his life consist"** (follows LOGOS — precise and clear) |

### 12:16 — *euphoresen*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "bore-well" |
| LOGOS | "brought forth plentifully" |
| PNEUMA | "produced an exceptional harvest" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"brought forth plentifully"** (follows LOGOS — natural English for NT hapax) |

### 12:17 — "crops" / "fruits"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "fruits" |
| LOGOS | "crops" |
| PNEUMA | "crops" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"crops"** (follows LOGOS/PNEUMA 2-to-1 — agricultural context) |

### 12:20 — *aphron*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "Senseless one" |
| LOGOS | "Fool" |
| PNEUMA | "You fool" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Fool"** (follows LOGOS — direct, consistent with 11:40 decision) |

### 12:20 — "soul is required" / "they demand your soul"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "they demand your soul from you" |
| LOGOS | "your soul is required of you" |
| PNEUMA | "your life is being demanded back from you" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"your soul is required of you"** (follows LOGOS — formal divine passive) |

### 12:21 — "stores up treasure" / "treasuring-up"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "treasuring-up" |
| LOGOS | "stores up treasure" |
| PNEUMA | "stockpiles wealth" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"stores up treasure"** (follows LOGOS — natural prose) |

### 12:25 — *helikian* — "stature" / "life"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "stature" |
| LOGOS | "stature" |
| PNEUMA | "life" (lifespan) |
| **SOPHIA** | **"stature"** (follows EMET/LOGOS 2-to-1 — preserves ambiguity with *pechyn*) |

### 12:27 — "lilies" / "wildflowers"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "lilies" |
| LOGOS | "lilies" |
| PNEUMA | "wildflowers" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"lilies"** (follows EMET/LOGOS 2-to-1 — established rendering of *krina*) |

### 12:27 — Solomon's clothing verb
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "clothed himself" |
| LOGOS | "was arrayed" |
| PNEUMA | "was dressed" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"was arrayed"** (follows LOGOS — formal dignity for royal context) |

### 12:28 — Address to disciples
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "ones of-little-faith" |
| LOGOS | "O you of little faith" |
| PNEUMA | "What little faith you have!" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"O you of little faith"** (follows LOGOS — vocative with appropriate solemnity) |

### 12:29 — *meteorizesthe*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "do not be anxious" |
| LOGOS | "do not be of anxious mind" |
| PNEUMA | "stop being anxious" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"do not be of anxious mind"** (follows LOGOS — distinguishes hapax from common *merimnao*) |

### 12:31 — "But" / "Nevertheless" / "Instead"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "Nevertheless" |
| LOGOS | "But" |
| PNEUMA | "Instead" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"But"** (follows LOGOS — concise adversative for *plen*) |

### 12:32 — *eudokesen*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "was well-pleased" |
| LOGOS | "it was your Father's good pleasure" |
| PNEUMA | "your Father has gladly chosen" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"it was your Father's good pleasure"** (follows LOGOS — theologically precise) |

### 12:35 — Girded loins imagery
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "Let your loins be girded" |
| LOGOS | "Let your loins be girded" |
| PNEUMA | "Stay dressed and ready for action" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Let your loins be girded"** (follows EMET/LOGOS 2-to-1 — preserves Passover allusion) |

### 12:36 — "wedding feast"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "wedding-feasts" (plural) |
| LOGOS | "wedding feast" |
| PNEUMA | "wedding banquet" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"wedding feast"** (follows LOGOS — natural singular) |

### 12:37 — "servants" / "slaves"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "slaves" |
| LOGOS | "servants" |
| PNEUMA | "servants" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"servants"** (follows LOGOS/PNEUMA 2-to-1) |

### 12:39 — *dioruchthēnai*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "dug-through" |
| LOGOS | "broken into" |
| PNEUMA | "broken into" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"broken into"** (follows LOGOS/PNEUMA 2-to-1 — natural English) |

### 12:42 — *oikonomos*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "steward" |
| LOGOS | "steward" |
| PNEUMA | "manager" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"steward"** (follows EMET/LOGOS 2-to-1 — established theological term) |

### 12:42 — *sitometrion*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "portion-of-grain" |
| LOGOS | "portion of food" |
| PNEUMA | "food allowance" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"portion of food"** (follows LOGOS — clear without hyphenation) |

### 12:45 — "menservants and maidservants"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "male-servants and female-servants" |
| LOGOS | "menservants and maidservants" |
| PNEUMA | "servants, both men and women" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"menservants and maidservants"** (follows LOGOS) |

### 12:46 — *dichotomēsei*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "cut-him-asunder" |
| LOGOS | "cut him asunder" |
| PNEUMA | "cut him to pieces" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"cut him asunder"** (follows LOGOS — severe without overstatement) |

### 12:47 — "stripes" / "blows" / "beating"
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "blows" |
| LOGOS | "stripes" |
| PNEUMA | "beating" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"stripes"** (follows LOGOS — formal traditional rendering) |

### 12:49 — *ti thelo ei ede anephthe*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "what do I wish if it has already been kindled?" |
| LOGOS | "how I wish it were already kindled!" |
| PNEUMA | "how I wish it were already ablaze!" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"how I wish it were already kindled!"** (follows LOGOS — exclamatory reading of difficult Greek) |

### 12:50 — *synechomai*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "pressed-together" |
| LOGOS | "constrained" |
| PNEUMA | "under pressure" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"constrained"** (follows LOGOS — precise single word) |

### 12:55 — *kauson*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "burning-heat" |
| LOGOS | "scorching heat" |
| PNEUMA | "scorching hot" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"scorching heat"** (follows LOGOS — noun form without hyphen) |

### 12:56 — *ton kairon touton*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "this season" |
| LOGOS | "this present time" |
| PNEUMA | "the present moment" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"this present time"** (follows LOGOS — eschatological weight of *kairos*) |

### 12:58 — *archon* in judicial context
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "ruler" |
| LOGOS | "magistrate" |
| PNEUMA | "magistrate" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"magistrate"** (follows LOGOS/PNEUMA 2-to-1 — precise legal term) |

### 12:58 — *dos ergasian*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "give effort" |
| LOGOS | "give diligence" |
| PNEUMA | "make every effort" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"give diligence"** (follows LOGOS — formal register) |

### 12:58 — *praktor*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "officer" |
| LOGOS | "officer" |
| PNEUMA | "bailiff" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"officer"** (follows EMET/LOGOS 2-to-1) |

### 12:59 — Emphatic negation *ou me*
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| EMET | "you shall not come out" |
| LOGOS | "you shall by no means come out" |
| PNEUMA | "you will never get out" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"you shall by no means come out"** (follows LOGOS — preserves emphatic *ou me*) |

## Summary Statistics

| Category | Count |
|---|---|
| Total disagreements logged | 38 |
| SOPHIA follows LOGOS | 29 |
| SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS (2-to-1) | 7 |
| SOPHIA follows LOGOS/PNEUMA (2-to-1) | 2 |
| SOPHIA follows EMET | 0 |
| SOPHIA follows PNEUMA | 0 |

### Pattern Notes

LOGOS dominates as the primary source for SOPHIA's consensus in Luke 12, reflecting the chapter's heavily didactic and eschatological content where formal equivalence provides the clearest rendering. EMET's hyper-literal constructions (excessive hyphenation, bracketed insertions, Semitic calques like "confess in me") were consistently set aside in favor of LOGOS's formal clarity. PNEUMA's dynamic renderings, while communicatively effective, introduced interpretive expansions ("wildflowers," "stockpiles wealth," "cut him to pieces," "Friend") that go beyond the Greek source. The chapter's inclusio structure (hypocrisy in 12:1 and 12:56) and its dense concentration of hapax legomena (*euphoresen*, *meteorizesthe*, *sitometrion*, *diamerismon*, *praktor*) required consistent formal precision throughout.

---

*Disagreements compiled by SOPHIA for Luke Chapter 12.*
